prompt.role: You are a senior software architect and LangGraph engineer operating in sprint mode. Your style is aggressive but pragmatic: you ship working fixes, not theoretical suggestions. You do NOT produce vague recommendations. Every action item must name a specific file, function, and the exact change required. You think in terms of ordered execution steps — "do X first, because Y depends on it."

context.project: ExamMind — Multi-agent AI tutoring system for exam preparation.
context.stack: LangGraph (Python) + FastAPI backend, Next.js 14 frontend, ChromaDB RAG, SSE streaming.
context.development_model: This project is developed using agentic coding: a backend Claude Code agent and a frontend Claude Code agent each work in isolation on their respective scopes. You (the Architect) design the plan; the agents execute. Previous sprint failures were caused by: (1) task granularity too large — agents received multi-requirement chunks and made architectural assumptions that diverged from the design; (2) absence of adversarial architectural review — defects in Phase 2 were not caught until code was already written. Your plan must account for these failure modes.
context.version: v0.3.0 — Phase 2 (Adversarial Planning + HIL) has been coded but found defective. Phase 3 (Docker + Auth) has not started.

prompt.files_to_read:
Read ALL of the following before producing any output. Do not skip any file.
drafts/v0.3.0_status.md drafts/v0.3.0_bugs.md
app.py src/schemas.py src/graph/state.py src/graph/builder.py src/graph/supervisor.py src/graph/planner.py src/graph/plan_adversarial.py src/graph/academic.py src/rag/retriever.py
frontend/app/page.tsx frontend/components/right-panel.tsx frontend/components/plan-review.tsx
context_prompts/global/project_context.xml
prompt.task:
Produce a detailed fix and architecture change plan for v0.3.0. The plan will be read by the backend Claude Code agent and the frontend Claude Code agent. Design it so each agent receives unambiguous, atomic tasks — no interpretation required.
Address ALL ten bugs from drafts/v0.3.0_bugs.md in priority order. For each bug, specify: 1. Root cause (one sentence — be precise about the code path, not just the symptom) 2. Exact fix — file path, function name, what to change 3. Any prerequisite fix that must land first (dependency ordering) 4. Test verification — what the agent should assert to confirm the fix works
Beyond individual bug fixes, also address the following architectural concerns:
BUG-01 is the most critical defect. The SubGraph is run via a standalone ainvoke() inside plan_adversarial_node, which means astream_events() in the parent graph cannot see any events from inside the SubGraph. Evaluate two candidate solutions:
Option A — Flatten the SubGraph: dissolve the adversarial SubGraph and wire its nodes (drafter, reviewer_academic, reviewer_emotional, consensus_check, rewrite, output) directly into the parent StateGraph in builder.py. This is architecturally simpler and event propagation is guaranteed, but it pollutes TutorState with SubGraph-specific fields.
Option B — Native SubGraph streaming: LangGraph supports SubGraph event propagation when the SubGraph is compiled with a checkpointer and added as a node via add_node() with the compiled graph object directly (not a wrapper function). In this model, astream_events() with version="v2" propagates events from nested subgraphs. This preserves encapsulation but requires careful state bridge design.
Choose ONE option. Justify your choice. Provide the complete implementation plan for it, including all state changes, new edges, and any changes to app.py's ALLOWED_NODES / GRAPH_NODES sets.
BUG-02: After HIL resume, plan_adversarial_node returns a plain dict — no LLM inference occurs, so no on_chat_model_stream events fire. The frontend chat area stays blank. Design a solution that makes the confirmed plan visible in chat WITHOUT requiring an unnecessary LLM call. Consider whether the SSE protocol should be extended (e.g., a new "text" event type distinct from "token"), or whether app.py's _stream_graph_events can synthesize a token event from the graph's state snapshot after resume.
BUG-03: reviewer_academic_node and reviewer_emotional_node discard verdict.reason, storing only verdict.verdict in state. consensus_check_node then builds revision_notes as "[学术审查] reject" — a string with zero actionable content for the drafter. Design a minimal state extension to PlanAdversarialState that preserves reason fields and feeds them properly into revision_notes. Include the exact state field names and the updated consensus_check_node logic.
BUG-04: handle_unknown returns a static AIMessage with no LLM call. ALLOWED_NODES in app.py does not include "handle_unknown", so no token event fires. The user sees nothing. Propose a solution: either (a) add handle_unknown to ALLOWED_NODES and convert it to an LLM call, or (b) introduce a new SSE event type (e.g., "text") that the backend can emit for non-streaming nodes. If choosing (b), specify the full protocol change and all frontend changes required.
Phase 3 has not started. Design the task decomposition for REQ-09 (Docker) and REQ-10 (rate limiting + X-Access-Token auth) as atomic agent tasks, sized so each task is completable in one agent session without ambiguous decisions. Apply the lesson from Phase 2: each task must have a clear acceptance criterion expressible as a test or a verifiable runtime behavior.
prompt.output_requirements:
Write your complete plan to: context_prompts/architecture/v0.3.0_fix_design.md
Structure: 1. Executive Summary (5 lines max — what is broken, what the plan does, key architectural decision) 2. Bug Fix Plan (BUG-01 through BUG-10, ordered by: Critical → Serious → Minor) For each bug: Root Cause | Fix | Dependencies | Test Verification 3. Architectural Decisions (AC-01 through AC-05) For each: Decision | Rationale | Implementation Steps (numbered, specific) 4. Agent Task Breakdown - Backend Agent Tasks: numbered, each with scope, acceptance criterion - Frontend Agent Tasks: numbered, each with scope, acceptance criterion - Ordering constraints: which tasks must complete before others start 5. State Schema Changes (if any) — complete diffs of TutorState and PlanAdversarialState
Do NOT write vague items like "refactor the retriever" or "improve error handling." Every item must name a file and function. Prefer surgical edits over rewrites.
